name: Question Time Brief
description: Prepare a QTB for Senate Estimates, a ministerial doorstop, or a stakeholder Q&A. High-stakes, time-sensitive format with built-in scrutiny testing.

inputs_required.name: topic
inputs_required.label: Topic
inputs_required.description: The topic the QTB is being prepared for
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. NDIS cost growth, robodebt settlement

inputs_required.name: forum
inputs_required.label: Forum
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Senate Estimates, media doorstop, stakeholder Q&A

inputs_required.name: audience
inputs_required.label: Audience
inputs_required.placeholder: e.g. Minister, Deputy Secretary, SES Band 2

inputs_required.name: length
inputs_required.label: Target length
inputs_required.description: How long should the QTB be?

inputs_required.name: key_documents
inputs_required.label: Reference materials (optional)
inputs_required.description: Paste in publicly available background — recent media coverage, published reports, Hansard extracts. Do not paste OFFICIAL: Sensitive content into public AI tools.
inputs_required.placeholder: Paste relevant publicly available content here...

steps.name: Context & Likely Questions
steps.description: Maps the issue, identifies likely question angles, hard questions, and key messages before drafting.
steps.user_action_after: Review the likely questions and add any from your own knowledge. Your read on what's politically sensitive is more accurate than the model's. Add or remove questions before moving on.

steps.name: Draft QTB
steps.description: Drafts the full QTB in standard APS format — key messages, background, talking points, what to avoid.
steps.user_action_after: Read the draft as if you are the reader about to walk into the forum. Does it cover the hard questions? Are the Key Messages actually usable? Note anything that feels wrong or that you couldn't defend.

steps.name: Claim Verification
steps.description: Extracts and categorises every factual claim in the draft. In a QTB, unverified statistics can cause serious problems under questioning.
steps.user_action_after: Check every flagged claim against an authoritative source before the brief is used. In a QTB context, a challenged fact mid-session is a serious problem. Resolve all HIGH RISK items.

steps.name: Parliamentary Scrutiny Test
steps.description: Stress-tests the brief from a hostile questioner's perspective — surfaces gaps in talking points before the reader faces a real one.
steps.user_action_after: Review the critique. Address the Top 3 fixes. Some issues may not apply to your context — use your judgment. The 'trap question' is the one most worth preparing a response to.

steps.name: Tighten & Finalise
steps.description: Revises the QTB incorporating verification and scrutiny feedback. Tightens Key Messages and talking points.
steps.user_action_after: Do a final read aloud — talking points in particular should sound natural for the reader to say. Resolve any remaining [VERIFY] or [INSERT DATA] items before the brief is used.

beginner.notes: Start with the context analysis and draft, then run claim verification before finalising. The parliamentary scrutiny test adds value but can be done after you're comfortable with the basic workflow.

intermediate.notes: Run the full workflow. Step 4 (scrutiny test) is where the model earns its keep — the critique is usually more useful than the draft itself.

advanced.notes: Run Step 4 multiple times with different personas (opposition senator, crossbencher, journalist). Consider running QG-TRUST-001 on the final revised brief as well as the draft.

claude.setup_guidance: Create a Claude Project called 'Parliamentary Preparation'. Paste the instructions below into the Project Instructions field. Upload standing context -- previous QTBs, committee Hansard, answers to questions on notice, and your agency's QTB template -- to the Project knowledge base. They will be available in every conversation automatically.
claude.content:
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level, specialising in parliamentary and ministerial advice.

You write in formal APS style -- concise, accurate, and politically aware. You understand the format and conventions of Question Time Briefs, including the Q&A structure, the importance of defensible positions, and the need for consistency with on-the-record statements.

You are familiar with:
- Senate Estimates procedures and conventions
- Ministerial correspondence and briefing conventions
- Accountability obligations under the PGPA Act
- Information-handling requirements under the PSPF and Privacy Act

When drafting content:
- Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring human input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not fabricate statistics, program details, legislative references, or named individuals' roles
- If you are uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly
- Keep answers concise and defensible -- a minister or senior official must be able to read them under pressure
claude.document_handling: Upload reference documents -- previous QTBs, committee Hansard, answers to questions on notice, and position papers -- to the Project knowledge base. They will be available in every conversation within the Project without re-uploading.

chatgpt.setup_guidance: Create a ChatGPT Project called 'Parliamentary Preparation'. Paste the instructions below into the Project instructions field. These apply to all conversations within the Project. Upload reference documents -- previous QTBs, Hansard extracts -- to the Project.
chatgpt.content:
## Role
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level, specialising in parliamentary and ministerial advice.

Write in formal APS style -- concise, accurate, and politically aware. You understand QTB format and conventions, including the Q&A structure and the need for defensible, on-the-record consistency.

## Standards
You are familiar with Senate Estimates procedures, ministerial correspondence conventions, PGPA Act accountability obligations, and PSPF and Privacy Act requirements.

## Rules
- Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps requiring human input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not fabricate statistics, legislative references, or program details
- If you are uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly
- Keep answers concise and defensible -- they must hold up under scrutiny
- Avoid excessive bullet points; use flowing prose for context sections
chatgpt.document_handling: Upload reference documents (previous QTBs, Hansard, answers to questions on notice) to the ChatGPT Project. Reference them by name in your prompts for best results.

copilot.setup_guidance: Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste the preferences below. These persist across all Copilot conversations. For detailed workflow context, paste it at the top of your first message. Stay in a single conversation for the full workflow.
copilot.content: I am an APS policy officer preparing parliamentary and ministerial documents. Write in formal APS style -- concise, accurate, and defensible. Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY] and information gaps with [INSERT DATA]. Do not invent statistics, program details, or legislative references.
copilot.document_handling: Keep reference documents (previous QTBs, Hansard) in SharePoint or OneDrive. Use the / key in Copilot to reference them in your prompts. Stay in a single conversation for all workflow steps.

other.setup_guidance: Paste the instructions below at the start of each new conversation before running the workflow steps. This establishes the context and constraints your AI tool should follow throughout the session.
other.content:
Role: You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) policy analyst at the EL1 level, specialising in parliamentary and ministerial advice.

Instructions: Write in formal APS style -- concise, accurate, and defensible. Follow standard QTB format conventions.

Rules:
- Flag uncertain claims with [VERIFY]
- Flag gaps needing input with [INSERT DATA]
- Do not invent statistics, legislative details, or program specifics
- If uncertain about a fact, say so explicitly
- Keep answers concise -- they must hold up under scrutiny
other.document_handling: Paste relevant document content (previous QTBs, Hansard extracts, position papers) directly into the prompts at the steps where you need to reference them.

known_limitations: Cannot know the reader's history with a committee, past commitments, or agency-specific intelligence.
Statistics, program names, and budget figures are high-risk for hallucination — verify all before any brief is used in a high-stakes forum.
This workflow produces AI-assisted drafts. You remain the accountable officer for the advice.
